questions.question: Explain the concept of runtime polymorphism in C++ with an example.
keyPoints: Runtime polymorphism is achieved through virtual functions
Function call resolved at runtime based on actual object type
Example: Base pointer pointing to derived object calls derived version

questions.question: What is a pure virtual function? How does it differ from a virtual function?
keyPoints: Pure virtual function declared with = 0 syntax
Has no implementation in base class
Makes class abstract - cannot be instantiated
Regular virtual function has implementation and derived class can optionally override

questions.question: Explain the vtable and vptr mechanism in C++ for virtual function resolution.
keyPoints: vtable is a lookup table per class containing virtual function addresses
vptr is a hidden pointer in each object pointing to class vtable
Constructor initializes vptr during object creation
Runtime calls use vptr to find correct function in vtable

questions.question: Why are virtual destructors necessary in C++? Give an example scenario.
keyPoints: Ensures proper cleanup when derived object deleted through base pointer
Without virtual destructor, only base destructor called causing memory leak
Essential in polymorphic base classes with dynamic memory

questions.question: What is object slicing? How can it be prevented?
keyPoints: Slicing occurs when derived object assigned to base object by value
Derived portion is lost leaving only base portion
Prevented by using pointers or references to base class

questions.question: Explain the purpose of override and final specifiers in C++11.
keyPoints: override ensures function actually overrides a base virtual function
Catches compile-time errors if override doesn't match any base function
final prevents function from being overridden in derived classes
final can also prevent class from being inherited

questions.question: What is the difference between early binding and late binding?
keyPoints: Early binding resolves function calls at compile time
Late binding resolves function calls at runtime using vtable
Virtual functions use late binding, non-virtual use early
Late binding enables polymorphism but has runtime overhead

questions.question: Design a class hierarchy for a Shape drawing application demonstrating virtual functions. Include a base class Shape with derived classes Circle and Rectangle. Implement appropriate virtual functions for calculating area.
keyPoints: Base class Shape with virtual function area()
Derived Circle class with radius and area implementation
Derived Rectangle class with length, breadth and area implementation
Demonstrate polymorphism with base pointer to derived objects
Show pure virtual function usage if Shape is made abstract

questions.question: Explain the rules for virtual functions in C++. Discuss overriding, accessibility, return types, and special cases like constructors and static functions.
keyPoints: Virtual functions can be overridden in derived classes with same signature
Overriding function cannot have more restrictive access
Covariant return types allowed (pointer/reference to derived class)
Constructors cannot be virtual, destructors should be virtual
Static functions cannot be virtual - no this pointer
Virtual functions can have default arguments - base version used if call through base pointer

questions.question: What is an abstract class? How is it implemented in C++? Explain with a real-world example where abstract classes are useful.
keyPoints: Abstract class contains at least one pure virtual function
Cannot be instantiated - only used as base class
Provides interface/contract for derived classes
Real-world example: Payment base class with processPayment() as pure virtual
Derived classes implement specific payment methods like CreditCard, PayPal

questions.question: Write a C++ program demonstrating polymorphism through virtual functions. Create a base class Animal with derived classes Dog and Cat. Use base class pointer to call derived class functions.
keyPoints: Base class Animal with virtual function speak()
Derived class Dog overriding speak() to print bark
Derived class Cat overriding speak() to print meow
Base pointer to Dog and Cat objects demonstrating polymorphism
Proper use of virtual destructor
